item.title: Drua Women target strong start against Brumbies
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 19/06/2026 The McDonald's Fiji Drua Women are looking to make a strong start when they host the Brumbies in Ba tomorrow in round 3 of the Super Rugby Women's competition.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 19/06/2026

The McDonald's Fiji Drua Women are looking to make a strong start when they host the Brumbies in Ba tomorrow in round 3 of the Super Rugby Women's competition.

Coach Mike Legge says the team reviewed last weekend's match and identified their slow start as an area that needs improvement.

While he acknowledged the side's resilience in coming from behind to beat the Force, Legge says they cannot afford to rely on another comeback against the Brumbies.

He adds that the team has also been working on tightening its defence around the ruck.

The coach says all the girls are fit and healthy and ready to roll tomorrow.

The Drua Women and the Brumbies are both on four points in the standings, while the Waratahs sit at the top of the table with eight points.

The Drua Women will face the Brumbies at 2.05pm tomorrow at 4R Stadium in Ba.

item.title: Canada secure dominant 6-0 victory over Qatar in Group B clash
item.description: Canada produced a dominant performance to defeat Qatar 6-0 in their FIFA World Cup Group B clash. The Canadians were already in complete control by halftime, taking a comfortable 3-0 lead into the break through goals from Cyle Larin and Jonathan David, who scored twice in the opening half.
item.content:encoded:
Canada produced a dominant performance to defeat Qatar 6-0 in their FIFA World Cup Group B clash.

The Canadians were already in complete control by halftime, taking a comfortable 3-0 lead into the break through goals from Cyle Larin and Jonathan David, who scored twice in the opening half.

Qatar's task became even more difficult after Homam El Amin was sent off in the 33rd minute, leaving his side to play most of the match with 10 men.

Canada continued to pile on the pressure in the second half as Niko Saliba added a fourth goal before an own goal from Mohammad Manai further increased the lead.

Jonathan David completed a memorable night by scoring his third goal in stoppage time to secure a hat-trick and seal Canada's biggest win of the tournament so far.

The emphatic victory boosts Canada's chances of progressing from Group B, while Qatar will now need a strong response in their remaining matches.

item.title: Full house expected as Hurricanes host Chiefs in Super Rugby final tomorrow
item.description: The Hurricanes have received a major boost ahead of tomorrow's Shop N Save Super Rugby Pacific Grand Final against the Chiefs, with several key players returning as they chase their first title in a decade. In their first grand final appearance since winning their inaugural championship 10 years ago, the Hurricanes have been able to name a near full-strength side, including Cam Roigard and Ruben L
item.content:encoded:
The Hurricanes have received a major boost ahead of tomorrow's Shop N Save Super Rugby Pacific Grand Final against the Chiefs, with several key players returning as they chase their first title in a decade.

In their first grand final appearance since winning their inaugural championship 10 years ago, the Hurricanes have been able to name a near full-strength side, including Cam Roigard and Ruben Love in the halves, co-captain Jordie Barrett and Billy Proctor in midfield, and Fehi Fineanganofo, Josh Moorby and Callum Harkin in the back three.

The Wellington-based side has also welcomed back blindside flanker Devan Flanders, who returns from concussion to join co-captain Du'Plessis Kirifi and Peter Lakai in the loose forward trio.

The Chiefs, meanwhile, have been forced into two changes in the backline, with Lalakai Foketi and Isaac Hutchinson making way for Kyle Brown at centre and Leroy Carter on the wing.

Saturday's final at Sky Stadium, commonly known as the Cake Tin, will be played before a capacity crowd after tickets sold out in just 15 minutes.

Hurricanes head coach Clark Laidlaw says the occasion was one the team had been building towards all season.

Laidlaw says the return of Flanders and the ability to field a settled side at such a crucial stage of the season was a significant advantage.

The Chiefs head into the decider after a dominant semi-final victory over the Crusaders, while the Hurricanes comfortably accounted for the Blues to secure hosting rights for the final.

Despite missing influential loose forward Wallace Sititi through concussion, Chiefs coach Jonno Gibbes said his side had earned the opportunity to compete for the title through consistent performances throughout the season.

The Chiefs are chasing their first Super Rugby title since 2013 and are appearing in their fourth grand final in as many seasons.

The final will also mark a personal milestone for Chiefs loose forward Simon Parker, who is set to make his 50th appearance for the club.

The Chiefs will face the Hurricanes at 7.05pm tomorrow.

item.title: Canada storm to 3-0 lead over Qatar at halftime in Group B clash
item.description: Canada are in complete control of their FIFA World Cup Group B clash against Qatar, leading 3-0 at halftime. Cyle Larin opened the scoring in the 16th minute before Jonathan David doubled Canada's advantage in the 29th minute.
item.content:encoded:
Canada are in complete control of their FIFA World Cup Group B clash against Qatar, leading 3-0 at halftime.

Cyle Larin opened the scoring in the 16th minute before Jonathan David doubled Canada's advantage in the 29th minute.

Qatar's hopes of a comeback suffered a setback when Homam El Amin was sent off in the 33rd minute, leaving his side with 10 men.

David then grabbed his second goal of the match in first-half stoppage time to give Canada a comfortable three-goal cushion heading into the break.

The result, if maintained, would put Canada in a strong position in Group B as they chase a place in the knockout stages.

item.title: Hurricanes and Chiefs set for Super Rugby Pacific title showdown
item.description: The tickets for the final were sold out in 15 minutes, guaranteeing a packed stadium and a vibrant atmosphere in Te Whanganui-ā-Tara for what promises to be an unforgettable championship clash. The Hurricanes and Chiefs will meet in at 7.05pm tomorrow.
item.content:encoded:
The tickets for the final were sold out in 15 minutes, guaranteeing a packed stadium and a vibrant atmosphere in Te Whanganui-ā-Tara for what promises to be an unforgettable championship clash.

The Hurricanes and Chiefs will meet in at 7.05pm tomorrow.

item.title: Switzerland beat Bosnia-Herzegovina 4-1 in Group B clash
item.description: Switzerland scored four second-half goals to defeat Bosnia-Herzegovina 4-1 in their international football match. After a scoreless first half, Switzerland broke the deadlock through Johan Manzambi in the 74th minute.
item.content:encoded:
Switzerland scored four second-half goals to defeat Bosnia-Herzegovina 4-1 in their international football match.

After a scoreless first half, Switzerland broke the deadlock through Johan Manzambi in the 74th minute.

Bosnia -Herzegovina 's troubles increased when Tarik Muharemović was shown a red card in the 80th minute, reducing his side to 10 men.

Ruben Vargas doubled Switzerland's lead four minutes later before Manzambi grabbed his second goal of the match in the 90th minute.

Edin Mahmić scored a late consolation goal for Bosnia-Herzegovina in stoppage time, but Granit Xhaka sealed the win for Switzerland with a penalty in the seventh minute of added time.

The victory strengthens Switzerland's campaign while Bosnia-Herzegovina were left to rue the red card that shifted momentum in the closing stages.

item.title: Switzerland and Bosnia-Herzegovina locked at 0-0 at half-time
item.description: Switzerland and Bosnia-Herzegovina are locked at 0-0 at half-time in their FIFA World Cup Group B clash. The match at Los Angeles Stadium remains evenly poised, with both sides yet to find the breakthrough as they head into the second half.
item.content:encoded:
Switzerland and Bosnia-Herzegovina are locked at 0-0 at half-time in their FIFA World Cup Group B clash.

The match at Los Angeles Stadium remains evenly poised, with both sides yet to find the breakthrough as they head into the second half.

Switzerland entered the match after a 1-1 draw with Qatar and are making their 13th FIFA World Cup appearance. Their best performance at the tournament remains reaching the quarter-finals.

Bosnia-Herzegovina, meanwhile, are making only their second FIFA World Cup appearance. They opened their Group B campaign with a 1-1 draw against Canada and are chasing their first-ever World Cup victory.

Meanwhile, South Africa fought back to earn a 1-1 draw against the Czech Republic in their FIFA World Cup clash this morning.

Canada will take on Qatar at 10am, while Mexico will meet South Korea at 1pm.

item.title: SJSS hoping to defend U-19 netball tittle
item.description: Defending Fiji Secondary Schools Netball champions Saint Joseph's Secondary School are aiming to retain their Under-19 title at this year's Fiji Secondary Schools Netball Tournament. The Saint Joseph Under-19 side did not feature in the opening round of competition at the Suva Grammar School grounds yesterday.
item.content:encoded:
Defending Fiji Secondary Schools Netball champions Saint Joseph's Secondary School are aiming to retain their Under-19 title at this year's Fiji Secondary Schools Netball Tournament.

The Saint Joseph Under-19 side did not feature in the opening round of competition at the Suva Grammar School grounds yesterday.

Meanwhile, after establishing itself as one of Fiji’s leading academic institutions for more than a decade, Jai Narayan College is now setting its sights on sporting success, with hopes of adding trophies to its achievements.

The school is participating in the Fiji Secondary Schools Netball Tournament for the first time, reflecting its commitment to the holistic development of its students.

School teacher and tournament organiser Master Pio Kerewaqa said that while Jai Narayan College has enjoyed significant academic success over the years, sporting accolades have been largely absent from the school’s trophy cabinet.

Kerewaqa says the school is determined to build on its strong academic reputation by encouraging greater participation in sports, beginning with netball.

He emphasised that sport plays a crucial role in developing students’ character, teaching valuable lessons in teamwork, discipline, leadership, and resilience that extend beyond the classroom.

According to Kerewaqa, the school’s involvement in the tournament forms part of a broader effort to provide students with a well-rounded education that promotes both academic excellence and physical development.

Through its participation in netball, Jai Narayan College hopes to cultivate a stronger sporting culture and celebrate success both in the classroom and on the court.

In the Under-17 grade, Jai Narayan College put up a strong performance against Saint Joseph Secondary School but went down 18-14.

Elsewhere, in the Under-15 division, Suva Grammar School defeated DAV Girls College 18-7.

In another Under-17 match, Saint Joseph Secondary School overcame Yat Sen Secondary School 16-11.

The Saint Joseph Secondary School Under-19 team did not play yesterday.

item.title: Late penalty earns South Africa 1-1 draw against Czech Republic
item.description: South Africa fought back to earn a 1-1 draw against the Czech Republic in their FIFA World Cup clash this morning.The Czech Republ

item.description: Colombia made a winning return to the FIFA World Cup by defeating debutants Uzbekistan 3-1.After failing to qualify for the 2022 c

item.title: Ghana secure dramatic 1-0 victory over Panama
item.description: Ghana have secured a dramatic 95th minute 1-0 victory over Panama in their opening FIFA World Cup Group L match.After a scoreless
